_doc: Pinned regression sites. Each site asserts: (a) geocoder hits the expected state, (b) listed elements have non-null coverage, (c) the overall score lands within tolerance, (d) the feasibility score lands within tolerance, (e) declared deal-killer signals are present/absent as expected. The 'tenant_profile' field (speculative|anchored|hyperscaler) reflects the actual or likely tenant type and conditions deal-killer probabilities. 'expected_outcome' (built|announced|unbuilt|shelved) is calibration metadata -- update docs/EFFICACY_SCORECARD.md when anchor outcomes change. Update intentionally; never to silence a real regression.

sites.notes: Active prospectus review (QueenAnne Realty). Pinned as speculative because developer, not anchor, initiated. Feasibility 'Strong' reflects adequate grid + tax + rate with only low-p Justice40 background risk.
ERCOT site, Stargate/OpenAI announcement. Hyperscaler profile. Strong rate + grid + tax; only Justice40 fires as background friction.

sites.address: 1101 Beech Rd SW, New Albany, OH 43054
sites.notes: The calibration anchor. New Albany is a built hyperscaler cluster (Meta, Amazon, Google, etc.). Legacy tool rated this 'Poor (0.07)' which contradicted reality -- triggered the 2026-04-20 recalibration: (a) cluster + brownfield-aware grid outlook (no killer fires); (b) hyperscaler tenant scaling so onerous_tariff_deposit (AEP Ohio Schedule DCT) drops 0.30 -> 0.045. 2026-04-24 (scorecard pass): Grid Access now derives from grid_outlook = promising (large AEP substation within 10 km + cluster), so opportunity reflects the same reality the killer side already encoded. Result: hyperscaler feasibility ~0.69 Good -- consistent with the site being actively under construction by tier-1 hyperscalers. The honest math now gives the same answer the world is giving.

Ashburn/Loudoun: largest DC market on earth (~127 existing DCs within 50 km). After 2026-04-24 screen review: cluster hub tier (>=1 GW built within 30 km) awards +0.20 opportunity (vs +0.12 before); marginal_nonattainment + justice40 retired as killers (narrative only). Net: feasibility ~0.70 Strong (was 0.60). Serves as the ceiling anchor for the scoring band.

Meta's Iowa anchor cluster. 15 nearby DCs (cluster signal on), adequate MidAmerican grid headroom, Iowa DC sales-tax exemption. High feasibility (0.68) consistent with multiple built phases. Calibration ceiling for Midwest cluster.
Google's Iowa cluster (Council Bluffs). 22 nearby DCs + MidAmerican grid + IA tax incentives. Mirror anchor to Altoona for Midwest hyperscaler build-out calibration.

known_gaps.service_territories: D5: HIFLD service territory dataset missing PacifiCorp/BPA for OR

sites.notes: Google's original Oregon cluster. Demonstrates that SDC-D seismic alone isn't a deal-killer (P=0.08 after calibration). Only 3 nearby DCs so cluster signal is off -- feasibility 0.52 Moderate relies on BPA HV line + large substation presence (power_outlook_doubtful does not trigger).

Rewritten 2026-04-20 (rev. after user-flagged Millsboro report showing Indian River substation). This site was the forcing function for three model additions: (1) RETIRED_GENERATION_SITES registry detects the 785 MW Indian River Power Plant 0.8 km away, suppressing grid_severely_insufficient since the switchyard + HV ties are repurposable; (2) REGULATORY_MORATORIUMS registry detects DE PSC 25-0826 (Delmarva interconnection pause, lifting late 2026) which is the TRUE primary risk; (3) moratorium killer split into utility_moratorium (tariff-level, P=0.90) vs regulatory_interconnection_pause (docket-level with defined lift, P=0.55, tenant-scaled). Expected: speculative tenant ~0.24 Challenging (matches revised Millsboro report's 'Viable with Conditions' framing despite Poor on legacy scale). Hyperscaler tenant scores ~0.39 Moderate. Use this site to test both brownfield and moratorium detection. 2026-04-24: overall_score raised 2.75 -> 3.35 because Grid Access scorecard factor now derives from grid_outlook (promising via Indian River brownfield) instead of max_mw heuristic; brownfield was previously visible to the killer suppression but invisible to the legacy scorecard.

Remote SW anchor. After 2026-04-24 grid-methodology rewrite: the site has no local supply anchor (no brownfield, no planned substation within 25 km, no >=230 kV substation within 10 km) but DOES have a 115 kV line 14 km away and the Southline 345 kV project at Hidalgo substation 42.7 km out. grid_outlook verdict = NEUTRAL (has HV access but no anchor). power_outlook_doubtful does NOT fire -- the old critical-tier P=0.75 was precisely the kind of false precision this rewrite retired (max_mw is a voltage-class heuristic, not a capacity study). Feasibility rose from ~0.10 to ~0.45 because we stopped penalizing 'ambiguous' as 'bad.' The narrative still surfaces that this is a thinly-served area; reading a neutral-outlook site is a diligence prompt, not a kill. If a future signal (e.g. a labeled backtest showing neutral-verdict NM/rural sites cancel at high rates) warrants a soft penalty for neutral, that's a separate decision.
